name: Bilbao

seo.title: Bilbao travel guide - stays, sights & 2026 events
seo.description: Plan Bilbao around the Guggenheim Museum, Casco Viejo, BIO airport links, hotel areas and local events.
seo.twitterDescription: Plan Bilbao around the Guggenheim Museum, Casco Viejo, BIO airport links, hotel areas and local events.
seo.webpageDescription: Plan Bilbao around the Guggenheim Museum, Casco Viejo, BIO airport links, hotel areas and local events.

heroText: Bilbao sits on the Nervion river in Spain's Basque Country, with the Guggenheim Museum, Casco Viejo pintxos streets, San Mames match nights and green hills close together. Use it as a compact city base for art, food, football, river walks and Basque coast day trips.

areas.name: Abando and Ensanche
areas.text: Choose Abando and Ensanche for the Guggenheim river walk, shopping streets, main station access, tram stops and a first Bilbao stay.

areas.name: Casco Viejo
areas.text: Choose Casco Viejo for old-town pintxos bars, the Seven Streets, Santiago Cathedral, market stops and a more historic evening base.

areas.name: Indautxu
areas.text: Choose Indautxu for San Mames, Azkuna Zentroa, restaurants, metro links and an easier balance between events and central sightseeing.

stayOverview: Compare Bilbao hotels around Abando, Ensanche, Casco Viejo or Indautxu before booking. The city is compact, but river walks, old-town evenings, San Mames events and airport transfer timing can point to different bases.
hotelIntro: Bilbao works best when the hotel base matches the repeated route: Guggenheim and the river, Casco Viejo nights, San Mames events, or an easy airport bus connection.
rentalCars: Use a rental car for Basque coast day trips, San Juan de Gaztelugatxe, Rioja Alavesa or mountain routes; keep the car out of the old-town core when possible.

travelTips.title: Best time to visit
travelTips.text: Spring and early autumn are good for river walks, museum time and Basque coast side trips without peak summer pressure.

travelTips.title: Transport notes
travelTips.text: Metro, tram and walking cover most central Bilbao routes; choose a hotel near the stop you will repeat most.

travelTips.title: Rain planning
travelTips.text: Bilbao has an Atlantic climate, so keep indoor time for the Guggenheim, Fine Arts Museum and food halls in the plan.

travelTips.title: Event nights
travelTips.text: For Athletic Club matches or arena events, compare Indautxu and San Mames-area stays against old-town evening plans.

highlights.title: Guggenheim Museum Bilbao
highlights.text: Frank Gehry's titanium museum anchors the riverfront, pairs art collections with outdoor sculpture and defines Bilbao's modern landmark route.

highlights.title: Casco Viejo
highlights.text: The old town's Seven Streets, Santiago Cathedral, Ribera Market and pintxos bars make Bilbao's most walkable historic evening core.

highlights.title: Zubizuri Bridge
highlights.text: The white pedestrian bridge crosses the Nervion river, linking central walks, museum routes, riverside photos and views toward the hills.

highlights.title: Bilbao Fine Arts Museum
highlights.text: The Fine Arts Museum adds a quieter art stop near Dona Casilda Iturrizar Park, central hotels and the Ensanche walking route.

highlights.title: Azkuna Zentroa
highlights.text: The converted wine warehouse is now a cultural centre with a distinctive columned atrium, exhibitions, cinema and public city-life spaces.

highlights.title: San Mames Stadium
highlights.text: Athletic Club's stadium is Bilbao's main sports landmark, useful for match-night atmosphere, event planning and Indautxu-area hotel choices.

nearbyIdeas.title: San Sebastian
nearbyIdeas.label: Basque coast
nearbyIdeas.text: A classic food and beach day trip from Bilbao when transport timing and hotel plans leave room for the coast.

nearbyIdeas.title: Getxo
nearbyIdeas.label: Metro coast
nearbyIdeas.text: Metro-linked waterfront, beaches and the Vizcaya Bridge area west of central Bilbao make an easy half-day route.

sources.label: Wikipedia: Bilbao
sources.usedFor: city history, coordinates and population context

history.text: Diego Lopez V de Haro founded Bilbao as a town on the Nervion estuary.
Bilbao received a consulate that strengthened its maritime trade role.

history.label: 19th century
history.text: Iron mining, port activity and industry made Bilbao one of northern Spain's major economic centres.
Bilbao fell during the Spanish Civil War after the Basque defensive line around the city was broken.
The Bilbao Metro opened, improving movement across the city and metropolitan area.
The Guggenheim Museum Bilbao opened and became the symbol of the city's post-industrial renewal.
